perturbation.original_text: This Agr eement will come into force on the [date of its execution]6 and will continue in 1 This is a standard template non disclosure agreement (sometimes called a “confidentiality agreement”). The template is suitable for use whe re one party is disclosing confidential information – for example, for the purpose of exploring whether to enter into partnership, a joint venture or another business arrangement or contract. 2 The date should be the date of signature; if the parties sign on different dates, it should be the date of the last signature. 3 Where a party to the contract is a sole trader or partnership rather than a company, the following party definitions may be used: sole trader: “[ INDIVIDUAL NAME ] trading as [ business n ame], which has its principal place of business at [address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” partnership: “[ PARTNERSHIP NAME ], a partnership established under [English] law having its principal place of business at [ address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” 4 You may wish to c hange the defined terms Disclose r and Recipient to suit the circumstances of your agreement. 5 It may be that only some of the terms are confidential. 6 Where Confidential Information has been disclosed before the date of execution ( i.e. signature by both parties) then you should consider making the agreement retrospective, and specifying here that the agreement came into force on a particular date before the date of execution. force [indefinitely, unless and until terminated in accordance with Clause [5] / until [ date] [event ], upon which it will terminate automatically, unless terminated in accorda nce with Clause [5]].
perturbation.changed_text: This Agr eement will come into force on the [date of its execution]6 and will continue for a period of 12 months. 1 This is a standard template non disclosure agreement (sometimes called a “confidentiality agreement”). The template is suitable for use whe re one party is disclosing confidential information – for example, for the purpose of exploring whether to enter into partnership, a joint venture or another business arrangement or contract. 2 The date should be the date of signature; if the parties sign on different dates, it should be the date of the last signature. 3 Where a party to the contract is a sole trader or partnership rather than a company, the following party definitions may be used: sole trader: “[ INDIVIDUAL NAME ] trading as [ business n ame], which has its principal place of business at [address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” partnership: “[ PARTNERSHIP NAME ], a partnership established under [English] law having its principal place of business at [ address ] (the “[ Disclose r/Recipient ]”)” 4 You may wish to c hange the defined terms Disclose r and Recipient to suit the circumstances of your agreement. 5 It may be that only some of the terms are confidential. 6 Where Confidential Information has been disclosed before the date of execution ( i.e. signature by both parties) then you should consider making the agreement retrospective, and specifying here that the agreement came into force on a particular date before the date of execution. force [indefinitely, unless and until terminated in accordance with Clause [5] / until [ date] [event ], upon which it will terminate automatically, unless terminated in accorda nce with Clause [5]].
perturbation.explanation: By changing the term of the agreement from 'indefinitely' or 'until a specific date/event' to a fixed period of '12 months', an in-text contradiction is introduced. While this clause specifies a definite term, Clause 5 still allows for termination by written notice, creating ambiguity about whether the agreement automatically ends after 12 months or can be terminated earlier, and then contradict the term definition on Section 1, where 'Term' means completion date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 [Either party] may termina te this Agreement [forthwith] at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies a fixed term of '12 months' for the agreement. However, the contradicted text (Clause 5.1) allows either party to terminate the agreement at any time with written notice. This creates a clear contradiction because the agreement is defined as both having a fixed term and being terminable at will, making the duration of the agreement uncertain and contradictory. The changed text alters the original text, causing a strong contradiction with the contradicted text. The change directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section, making enforcement unclear and creating a logical inconsistency as the parties can terminate the contract before the 12 months. This adheres to the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement];
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Proprietary Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement];
perturbation.explanation: By changing “Confidential Information” to “Proprietary Information”, this clause now refers to a term that is never defined, creating an in-text contradiction. The agreement only defines “Confidential Information.” This means the obligations in this clause are now ambiguous and potentially unenforceable because it's unclear what 'Proprietary Information' encompasses, specially considering the definition from Confidential Information is in place.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Proprietary Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement]; (b) that it will use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as it uses to protect its own confidential i nformation of a similar nature, being at least a reasonable degree of care; and [(c) that it will act in good faith at all times in relation to the Confidential Information, and will not use any of the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose.]
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which presumably is defined in the contract. The changed text replaces this with 'Proprietary Information,' which, according to the Perturbation Explanation, is not defined in the contract. The contradicted text also refers to 'Proprietary Information'. Because the term is undefined, there's ambiguity about what the Recipient is obligated to keep confidential, creating a contradiction. Furthermore, this aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclose r warrants to the Recipient th at it has the legal right and authority to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclose r represents to the Recipient th at it has the legal right and authority to enter into and perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'warrants' to 'represents' weakens the obligation. A warranty is a promise backed by a remedy, while a representation is a statement of fact. If the Discloser only 'represents' that they have the right to enter the agreement, the Recipient may have a harder time seeking damages if that representation turns out to be false. This is an in-text contradiction that reduces the Discloser's duty, by using different terminology for similar concept.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 All of the parties' warranties and representations in respect of the subject matter of this Agreement are expressly set out in the terms of this Ag reement. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, no other warranties or representations concerning the subject matter of this Agreement will be implied into this Agreement or any related contract.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'warrants' to 'represents' weakens the Discloser's obligation, as explained in the perturbation explanation. The contradicted text states that all warranties and representations are expressly set out in the agreement. By weakening 'warrants' to 'represents', it impacts the enforceability and nature of the obligations defined, creating a direct conflict and a strong in-text contradiction. The change narrows the Discloser's duty, thus breaking compatibility with the contradicted section's expectation of all warranties/representations being expressly set out.
